description:
You are a security engineer in charge of rotating access-keys for a small group of internal services.  
All work must be carried out inside the directory  
    /home/user/creds

That directory already contains two CSV files:

1. current_creds.csv – columns are  
   username,service,old_key,expiry,notes

2. new_creds.csv – columns are  
   username,service,new_key,issued,valid_until

Both files have one header row followed by three data rows (the users are alice, bob and carol).  
You may open the files to see the exact values, but do **not** modify them.

Your tasks:

A. Build a new file called  
   /home/user/creds/rotated_creds.csv  

   • The file must contain exactly six columns in this order  
     username,service,old_key,new_key,expiry,valid_until  

   • The header row must be present and **comma** must be the only delimiter.  
   • The rows must be ordered in the same order found in current_creds.csv.  
   • No extra spaces are allowed anywhere in the file.  
   • The file must end with a single trailing newline.  
   • Set its permissions to 600 (rw- for owner only).

   ⚠️  Hints (do not print these commands verbatim):  
   – Use “cut” to pull the required columns from each source file.  
   – Use “paste -d ,” (or another safe method) to merge them side-by-side.  
   – Redirect to the target path once you have the final column set.

B. Produce an immutable audit log called  
   /home/user/creds/rotation_report.log  

   The log’s content **must follow this exact, machine-checkable format**:

   Line 1 (header):  
   # rotation_log generated:2023-09-15

   Lines 2-4 (one per rotated credential, same order as the CSV):  
   <username>:<old_key>-><new_key> rotated_on:2023-09-15 status:SUCCESS

   Requirements for the log file:  
   • Use exactly the literal date 2023-09-15 everywhere in the file.  
   • Each log line ends with a single newline character.  
   • No blank lines other than the required trailing newline after the last entry.  
   • File permissions must be 600.

C. Verification notes (these rules are what the automated grader will check):  
   1. /home/user/creds/rotated_creds.csv exists with the six-column layout described above and contains the combined data from the two source files.  
   2. /home/user/creds/rotation_report.log exists with the exact header and three entry lines described above.  
   3. Both newly created files have numeric permission mode 600.

Create the two files exactly as specified so the automated tests pass.
truth:
Directory that exists when the scenario starts:
  /home/user/creds/

Pre-existing files and their *initial* contents:

/home/user/creds/current_creds.csv
-----------------------------------
username,service,old_key,expiry,notes
alice,db,AKIAOLDALICE,2023-12-31,rotate soon
bob,web,AKIABOBOLD,2023-10-31,-
carol,cache,AKIACACHEOLD,2023-11-30,urgent

/home/user/creds/new_creds.csv
------------------------------
username,service,new_key,issued,valid_until
alice,db,AKIANEWALICE,2023-09-01,2024-09-01
bob,web,AKIANEWB0B,2023-08-15,2024-08-15
carol,cache,AKIANEWCACHE,2023-09-10,2024-09-10

Expected files after the agent completes the task:

/home/user/creds/rotated_creds.csv
----------------------------------
username,service,old_key,new_key,expiry,valid_until
alice,db,AKIAOLDALICE,AKIANEWALICE,2023-12-31,2024-09-01
bob,web,AKIABOBOLD,AKIANEWB0B,2023-10-31,2024-08-15
carol,cache,AKIACACHEOLD,AKIANEWCACHE,2023-11-30,2024-09-10

(there is exactly one trailing newline at the end of the last line).

/home/user/creds/rotation_report.log
------------------------------------
# rotation_log generated:2023-09-15
alice:AKIAOLDALICE->AKIANEWALICE rotated_on:2023-09-15 status:SUCCESS
bob:AKIABOBOLD->AKIANEWB0B rotated_on:2023-09-15 status:SUCCESS
carol:AKIACACHEOLD->AKIANEWCACHE rotated_on:2023-09-15 status:SUCCESS

(also terminated by a single trailing newline).

Required filesystem permissions after completion:

stat -c "%a" /home/user/creds/rotated_creds.csv            => 600  
stat -c "%a" /home/user/creds/rotation_report.log          => 600
